summary: Build a single-file HTML page (KAN-125) that demonstrates Scrum ceremonies via two tabs: a 'Ceremonies' overview tab and a 'Why It Matters' explanatory tab. Delivered in two tasks per the ticket's explicit multi-step requirement.

epics.title: Scrum Ceremonies Single-File HTML Demo
epics.description: Produce one self-contained HTML file that educates users on Scrum ceremonies through two browser tabs: a 'Scrum Ceremonies' overview tab and a 'Why It Matters' explanation tab. Delivery is split into two sequential tasks per the ticket's critical instruction.

tasks.title: HTML scaffold and Scrum Ceremonies overview tab
tasks.description: Create the single-file HTML (`scrum-ceremonies.html`) with a two-tab navigation shell and populate the first tab ('Scrum Ceremonies') with a section for each of the five core Scrum ceremonies (Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective, Backlog Refinement), each containing its name, cadence, participants, and a short description. The file must be fully self-contained (inline CSS and JS, no external dependencies) and render correctly when opened from the local filesystem.
acceptance_criteria: File `scrum-ceremonies.html` exists in the repository root.
Opening the file in a browser shows two clickable tabs; the first tab is active by default.
The first tab displays all five Scrum ceremonies, each with name, cadence, participants, and a short description.
No external network requests are made (all styles and scripts are inline).
HTML is valid and renders without console errors in a modern browser.

tasks.title: Add 'Why It Matters' second tab to the Scrum Ceremonies HTML
tasks.description: Extend `scrum-ceremonies.html` to populate the second tab ('Why It Matters') with an explanation of the importance of each of the five ceremonies covered in the first tab. Each entry should state the team and business value delivered by the ceremony, written for someone new to Scrum. Tab-switching behavior must work correctly after this addition, and the file must remain fully self-contained.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the second tab displays a 'Why It Matters' view covering all five ceremonies.
Each ceremony has a distinct importance section explaining its team and business value.
Switching between tabs works without page reload and without JavaScript errors.
The file remains a single self-contained HTML file with no external dependencies.
Content on the second tab is distinct from (not a repetition of) the first tab's descriptions.
dependencies: HTML scaffold and Scrum Ceremonies overview tab
